title: Germany's fossil-rich limestone reveals a new 150-million-year-old flying reptile
summary_full: Scientists in Germany have identified a new ancient flying reptile species. This nearly complete fossil dates back approximately 150 million years. The creature, named Laueropterus vitriolus, had a wingspan of about one meter. Its preservation offers insights into ancient environments and prehistoric animal communities. This discovery highlights ongoing learning about prehistoric life globally.
